column1: # DOLCHEO+ encoding TSV — schema per canon/governance/writing-conventions.md
# Tab-separated. Session: 2026-06-02 deploy architecture (LOCKED). See canon/governance/deploy-architecture.md

column2: Lock a three-tier deploy (prod/staging/preview) on Cloudflare Workers Builds, preview as the shared preview backend
column3: One wrangler.toml, three envs, three Workers Builds projects (one per worker). prod (production branch -> transcode-mcp, class AudioContainer), staging (staging branch -> transcode-mcp-staging, class AudioContainerStaging), preview (preview branch -> transcode-mcp-preview, class AudioContainerPreview). Each tier its own DO class (container app keyed on DO class) and its own R2 bucket. preview is the single shared preview backend: every PR branch is a VERSION of transcode-mcp-preview via Workers Builds non-production branch build (wrangler versions upload), getting its own stable preview URL while sharing the one preview container/DO/bucket; last-build-wins on shared state, each version previews its own code. Deploys ALWAYS via Workers Builds (dashboard + githooks), NEVER GitHub Actions.
column4: wrangler.toml parses with prod + [env.staging] + [env.preview], three classes/buckets, [env.preview] removed; src/worker.ts exports AudioContainer + AudioContainerStaging + AudioContainerPreview; typecheck clean, 86 tests; CF docs build-branches confirms separate non-production-branch deploy command.
column5: Single preview worker (PR2 overwrites PR1). Per-branch container/DO (unbounded classes, collisions). GitHub Actions deploys (operator uses Workers Builds only). Cross-script branch->staging container (unverified).
column6: LOCKED — repo verified; operator wires 3 Workers Builds projects + preview branch/bucket

column2: A Cloudflare container application is keyed on the Durable Object class name, not the worker name
column3: Two workers cannot both own a container app for the same DO class (DURABLE_OBJECT_ALREADY_HAS_APPLICATION). Every full stack therefore needs its own class: AudioContainer (prod), AudioContainerStaging, AudioContainerPreview, each a trivial subclass exported from src/worker.ts; the binding NAME stays AUDIO_CONTAINER in every env.
column4: Repeated build failures DURABLE_OBJECT_ALREADY_HAS_APPLICATION naming class AudioContainer; resolved by distinct classes.
column5: Reuse one class across tiers (rejected: collision).

column2: wrangler versions upload cannot carry a NEW migration
column3: Preview/version uploads only work when the DO class + migration already exist on the worker. So the shared preview worker DO/migration is created once by a full preview-branch deploy; after that, code-only PR branches versions-upload fine (shared preview DO, last-build-wins on state). A PR that changes the DO shape introduces a new migration and is rejected on versions upload — the one known edge case; preview such a branch by deploying it to the preview branch (full deploy).
column4: CF Durable Objects docs: new Worker versions with new migrations cannot be uploaded; CF build-branches docs: non-production branch builds run a separate deploy command.
column5: Per-branch full deploy of a unique worker (rejected: container-app collision, no scale).

column2: Deploy mechanism is Cloudflare Workers Builds + githooks, never GitHub Actions
column3: Operator deploys exclusively via Workers Builds (dashboard connected to GitHub via githooks), which authenticates on the Cloudflare side — no GitHub repo secrets for Cloudflare, ever. A GitHub Actions deploy workflow was drafted and removed once corrected. Workers Builds pins a project to ONE worker and overrides the config worker name, so each tier needs its own project; running the wrong --env command in the prod project retargets prod.
column4: Prior deploy logs are Workers Builds (/opt/buildhome/...); operator correction; name-override warning Failed to match Worker name expected transcode-mcp Overriding; deploy.yml removed.

column2: Workers Builds Retry re-runs the ORIGINAL commit, not the latest
column3: After pushing a fix, retrying the failed build kept failing identically because Retry rebuilds the commit that build started on. Always trigger a NEW build on the latest commit (push or build-latest), never Retry, when validating a fix.
column4: Three consecutive identical failures across pushed fixes until a fresh build ran the new commit.
column5: Retry the failed build (rejected: rebuilds stale commit).

column2: Tiers must not share an R2 bucket because the cache is content-addressed with no recipe version
column3: The cache key is sha256(source+preset+q+codec) with no recipe/version component, so a build that changes transcode OUTPUT (codec/bitrate/quality tuning — frequent in this project) writes different bytes under an existing key; anyone sharing that bucket then serves those experimental bytes (cache poisoning). This is why there are THREE workers not two: prod and staging each need a clean bucket, and a connection's non-production (preview) lane shares the env worker bucket with its live version, so prod and staging keep preview lanes OFF. Only the preview tier shares one bucket across its branch versions (last-build-wins acceptable there). A recipe/version component in the key would let tiers share safely — not today's design.
column4: Project cache-key canon (sha256 of source+preset+q+codec, no version); operator decision 2026-06-02 to run all three tiers and avoid gymnastics.
column5: Two tiers with staging sharing prod or the preview bucket (rejected: poisons the clean gate / prod). Add a recipe-version to the key so tiers can share a bucket (deferred design change).

column4: DO-shape-changing PRs cannot be previewed as preview versions
column5: Because versions upload rejects new migrations, a PR that changes the Durable Object shape can't get a preview preview URL. Deferred by operator decision (fix when hit): preview such a branch by deploying it to the preview branch via full deploy, or bump the shared preview migration deliberately. Revisit if DO-shape PRs become common.
